name: Tomato Beef (Fon Kehr Ngow Yuk)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut flank steak into 3 long strips (cutting with the grain of the meat). Cut each strip into thin slices, cutting across the grain.
2. Add seasoning to beef and mix well.
3. Cut celery into 1 1/2-inch pieces; then, cut each piece lengthwise into strips, julienne style.
4. Remove seeds from bell peppers and cut into 1 1/2-inch pieces.
5. Cut each tomato into eights.
6. Heat wok and add 2 tbsp oil. Stir-fry celery and bell peppers for 3 minutes. Sprinkle lightly with salt and sugar. Remove and set aside.
7. Heat wok, add 2 tbsp oil, garlic, and beef. Stir-fry for 3 minutes; remove and set aside with vegetables.
8. Add chicken stock to wok, then vinegar, sugar, salt, and apple cider vinegar. Bring to a boil.
9. Add tomatoes, vegetables, and beef. Turn the heat to high and bring to a boil again.
10. Prepare thickening using cornstarch and cold water. Stir into the mixture, cook for 1 minute, and serve.
